figures.caption: Figure 1 – Graph of the stabilizer t→ ςα1,λ1,c1(t) (left) and 5 samples paths tk 7→ V 1 tk (right) over the time interval [0, 1], for the Hurst esponent H = 0.1, c1 = 0.01 and number of time steps n = 600.

Figure 2 – Graph of the stabilizer t→ ςα2,λ2,c2(t) (left) and 5 samples paths tk 7→ V 2 tk (right) over the time interval [0, 1], for the Hurst esponent H = 0.4, c2 = 0.03 and number of time steps n = 600.
Figure 4 – Graph of tk 7→ Var(V 2 tk ,M) and tk 7→ E[σ2(V 2 tk ,M)] over [0, 1], c2 = 0.03 and n = 600.

Figure 5 – Graph of tk 7→ ψ1 tk and tk 7→ ψ2 tk over [0, 1] with the fractional Adams algorithm and the number of time steps n = 600 both for correlation verifying |ρi| ≤ 1 2 (left) and |ρi| > 1 2 (right), i ∈ {1, 2}.
Figure 3 – Graph of tk 7→ Var(V 1 tk ,M) and tk 7→ E[σ2(V 1 tk ,M)] over [0, 1], c1 = 0.01 and n = 600.
Figure 6 – Graph of tk 7→ α∗ tk (left) and tk 7→ Xα∗ tk (right) over the time interval [0, T ], T = 1. Number of steps: n = 600.

Figure 7 – Statistics for strategies and wealth. Based on 5000 simulated paths, the solid line plots the mean and the shadow area is the 95% confidence interval estimated by bootstrapping. The terminal wealth is closer to the expected value m = 2.255. The parameters are the same as in Figure 6.

Figure 8 – Plots of the efficient frontier and variance. We set the parameters x0 = 2, T ∈ {0.5, 1.0, 5.0}, and m ∈ [ x0e (r+0.01)T , x0e (r+0.5)T ] . The solid curve corresponds to the theoretical frontier in (3.66), while the dotted curves represent the variance obtained from the Monte Carlo simulations of Xα∗ T .
